REPLY MEMO OF P & A IN OPPOS. TO MTN TO DISMISS OR STAY ACTION AND TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FILED BY DEFENDANT E T FINE ARTS INC., DBA MODERNISM GALLERY EMMETT, FREDERIC ("RIC")

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS E T FINE ARTS INC. AND FREDERIC EMMETT'S MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ACTION AND TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS DENIED. THE COURT HAS SPECIFIC PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANT FREDERIC EMMETT. EMMETT PURPOSEFULLY AVAILED HIMSELF OF THE FORUM BY AGREEING TO SELL AND SHIP THE TABLE TO CALIFORNIA, THE CONTROVERSY ARISES OUT OF EMMETT'S CONTACT WITH CALIFORNIA, AND THE ASSERTION OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION COMPORTS WITH FAIR PLAY AND SUBSTANTIAL JUSTICE. (PAVLOVICH V. SUPERIOR COURT (2002) 29 CAL.4TH 262, 269; SEE ALSO LUBERSKI, INC. V. OLEFICIO F.LLI AMATO S.R.L. (2009) 171 CAL.APP.4TH 409.) DEFENDANTS DID NOT MEET THEIR BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT TRYING THE CASE IN CALIFORNIA WOULD BE SERIOUSLY INCONVENIENT, BURDENSOME, AND PROHIBITIVELY COSTLY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action Adn To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Nov-12-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs E T FINE ARTS INC. and FREDERIC EMMETT's Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Action And To Quash Service Of Summons IS Denied. The Court has specific personal jurisdiction over Defendant Frederic Emmett. Emmett purposefully availed himself of the forum by agreeing to sell and ship the table to California, the controversy arises out of Emmett's contact with California, and the assertion of personal jurisdiction comports with fair play and substantial justice. (Pavlovich v. Superior Court (2002) 29 Cal.4th 262, 269; see also Luberski, Inc. v. Oleficio F.LLi Amato S.R.L. (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 409.) Defendants did not meet their burden of showing that trying the case in California would be seriously inconvenient, burdensome, and prohibitively costly. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2013 Continued To Nov-20-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
